LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANTS MASHA LEVINSON AND LEON TAYLOR'S MOTION FOR IMMEDIATE SALE OF REAL PROPERTY IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTIONS 872.120 AND 872.130 AND THE COURT'S BROAD EQUITABLE POWER IN PARTITION ACTIONS. THE PROPERTY LOCATED AT 461 AND 465 BELLEVUE AVENUE, OAKLAND, CALIFORNIA SHALL BE SOLD. THE PROCEEDS FROM THE SALE SHALL BE PLACED IN A BLOCKED ACCOUNT PENDING RESOLUTION OF THIS CASE. IT IS NOT NECESSARY FOR THE COURT TO DETERMINE THE PROPERTY INTERESTS OF THE PARTIES AND RIGHTS OF ANY LIENHOLDERS AT THIS TIME. IN ANY EVENT, IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT THE PARTIES' RESPECTIVE INTERESTS IN THE PROPERTY ARE DISPUTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. A STATUS HEARING RE: SALE OF PROPERTY/APPOINTMENT OF REFEREE IS SET FOR OCTOBER 25, 2012 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARMENT 302. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendants Masha Levinson and Leon Taylor's Motion For Immediate Sale Of Real Property is granted pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sections 872.120 and 872.130 and the Court's broad equitable power in partition actions.
